title: Race-Year Grouped Hazard Priors
summary: Random row CV can leak race strategy patterns because rows from the same race/driver trajectory appear in both train and validation.
rationale: Random row CV can leak race strategy patterns because rows from the same race/driver trajectory appear in both train and validation. F1 pit timing is a per-race hazard shaped by tyre rules, race progress, compound, and stint, so smoothed out-of-fold stop-lap priors should be learned under race-year grouping.
implementation_hint: Use GroupKFold by Year_Race or leave-one-race-out to build OOF target encodings for bins such as Race x Stint x Compound x LapNumber, Race x RaceProgressBin, Year x Race x Stint, and Compound x TyreLifeBin. Smooth toward global positive rate and add both exact-bin prior and cumulative hazard prior P(stop next lap | survived to this lap).
expected_effect: More leaderboard-robust ranking and possibly small CV gain if current CV is optimistic or noisy.
risk: May reduce local random-CV score while improving true generalization; sparse bins need strong smoothing.
